success_criterion: After implementation: stability_check.py --compare reports categorical magnitude/reversibility disagreement between two paired runs (handling both log schemas); SKILL.md Step 2 has a 2-line note about the 40% categorical flip risk; TODO reflects actual state (regex already done removed, #1-B marked done, #1-C/#1-D marked experiments-pending).
verification: python scripts/stability_check.py --compare .consilium/runs/2026-05-26_0030_todo-triage-cv.json .consilium/runs/2026-05-26_0100_consilium-data-folder.json outputs a 'Categorical stability' section; python scripts/run_evals.py exits 0.

candidates.summary: Extend stability_check.py to compare categorical fields; add SKILL.md Step 2 flip-rate note; clean up TODO.
candidates.sketch: In stability_check.py: add _categorical_fields(run) that extracts (magnitude, reversibility) from deliberation_log conservator step — handles both flat schema {step:conservator, reversibility:..., magnitude:...} and nested schema {step:conservator, scores:[{id, regression_risk:{reversibility, magnitude}}]}. For nested, pick the chosen_approach candidate's score. Extend compare() to print a 'Categorical stability' section showing magnitude/reversibility from each run and flagging disagreement. In SKILL.md Step 2: add 2-line note after Conservator output description: 'Categorical flip caveat: Conservator magnitude/reversibility labels have a 40% inter-run flip rate on ambiguous inputs (experiment 2026-05-17). If the deliberation is sensitive to the magnitude/reversibility boundary, consider double-sampling.' In TODO: remove regex fix item (already done in run_task.py line 191), mark #1-B done, update #1-C/#1-D to 'tooling ready, experiments pending'.
candidates.rationale: Addresses all three open items at once. The script change directly implements #1-B. The SKILL.md note makes the risk visible to orchestrators without mandating expensive double-sampling. The TODO cleanup reflects reality.
candidates.downside_estimate: ~60 lines of code change across 3 files. Moderate scope, low risk.

candidates.summary: Only extend stability_check.py for categorical comparison; no SKILL.md or TODO changes.
candidates.sketch: Same stability_check.py change as above. No SKILL.md edit. No TODO cleanup.
candidates.rationale: Minimal footprint. Script change is the core deliverable.
candidates.downside_estimate: SKILL.md remains silent on categorical flip risk. TODO remains stale on regex item.

candidates.summary: Run the actual stability experiments (#1-C/#1-D): pick 3 boundary candidates, re-run Consilium twice each, compare.
candidates.sketch: python scripts/stability_check.py --pick 3 to identify candidates. For each, dispatch a Consilium run sub-agent twice on the same input. Save as stability_a/b.json pairs. Compare with --compare. Document findings.
candidates.rationale: Provides actual empirical data for F4 gap and Generator/Control unanchored voices. No code change needed.
candidates.downside_estimate: Expensive: 6 full Consilium sub-agent dispatches. Results are data, not code — non-persistent unless saved to experiments/.

candidates.summary: Adversarial: script extension silently produces no categorical output if schema detection fails.
candidates.sketch: If _categorical_fields() returns None on both log schemas (e.g., chosen_approach not in conservator scores, or deliberation_log missing conservator step), compare() prints nothing for categorical section — false green. Mitigation: emit explicit 'MISSING — categorical fields not found in one or both runs' when detection fails.
candidates.rationale: Identifies the silent-failure risk in the script extension.
candidates.downside_estimate: If not mitigated: users see no categorical output and assume stability when data is actually missing.
TODO stays stale. #1-B unimplemented. Categorical flip risk invisible.

generator.fallback_scenario: If categorical comparison is too complex, user accepts script-only numeric comparison (already exists) and a SKILL.md note as the deliverable.

glossary.flat schema: deliberation_log conservator entry with reversibility/magnitude at top level: {step:conservator, reversibility:..., magnitude:...}
glossary.nested schema: deliberation_log conservator entry with per-candidate scores: {step:conservator, scores:[{id, regression_risk:{reversibility, magnitude}}]}
glossary.categorical flip: two independent Conservator runs on the same input produce different magnitude or reversibility labels (observed 40% rate for magnitude in 2026-05-17 experiment)
glossary.chosen_approach: the id of the winning candidate from aggregation — needed to pick which nested score to compare

hidden_assumptions.assumption: deliberation_log always contains a conservator step
hidden_assumptions.if_false_then: _categorical_fields returns None — must emit explicit MISSING message, not silent null

hidden_assumptions.assumption: chosen_approach in the report matches an id in conservator scores array (nested schema)
hidden_assumptions.if_false_then: categorical extraction fails for nested schema — must fall back to first score or emit MISSING

fixed_constraints: Must not break existing stability_check.py numeric comparison
Must handle both log schemas — no assumption about which one a run uses
negotiable_constraints: Whether SKILL.md note is 2 lines or a full sub-section

tests_to_write.name: compare two runs with known categorical fields produces categorical section
tests_to_write.assert: stability_check.py --compare run_a run_b stdout contains 'Categorical stability'

tests_to_write.name: compare handles missing conservator step gracefully
tests_to_write.assert: stability_check.py --compare run_missing_log run_b prints 'MISSING' not traceback

verdicts.notes: Must handle the adversarial_null_output case — emit MISSING when fields not found. Otherwise clean implementation.

issues.detail: Leaves SKILL.md silent on the 40% categorical flip risk — a known finding from the 2026-05-17 experiment.

verdicts.notes: Valid but incomplete — same script change, skips the docs note.

issues.detail: Requires 6 full Consilium sub-agent dispatches; results are ephemeral unless saved to experiments/. The tooling exists; this is purely a question of cost vs timing.

verdicts.notes: Valid but orthogonal to #1-B. Can be deferred — tooling is ready.
Correctly identifies the silent-failure risk. Mitigation is mandatory in extend_categorical_plus_cleanup.

counterparty_risks: Two log schemas may not cover all edge cases; future schema changes could break extraction silently
scores.bias_check: Not status quo bias — the 40% flip rate is a real measured gap.
rollback_recipe: git revert the stability_check.py and SKILL.md changes
scores.notes: Moderate scope (3 files, ~60 lines). Fully reversible. Main risk: schema detection edge cases — mitigated by explicit MISSING output.

scores.bias_check: Low risk, low value — does not close the SKILL.md documentation gap.

scores.bias_check: Running experiments now vs later is a timing tradeoff, not a real irreversibility.
scores.notes: Empirical work — valuable but detached from the code change goals. Better deferred to a dedicated experiment session.

scores.bias_check: Not status quo bias — this identifies a real silent-failure mode.
scores.notes: Adversarial candidate — correctly flags the risk that must be mitigated in the chosen approach.
